zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Beauchamp於大約1237年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾時，她的父親William de Beauchamp24歲，母親Isabel Mauduit24歲。她於1257年之前與Bartholomew de Sudeley結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1298年享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Beauchamp于大约1237年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾时，她的父亲William de Beauchamp24岁，母亲Isabel Mauduit24岁。她于1257年之前与Bartholomew de Sudeley结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1298年享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Beauchamp was born about 1237, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William de Beauchamp, was 24 and her mother, Isabel Mauduit, was 24. She married Bartholomew de Sudeley before 1257. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1298, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Beauchamp est née environ 1237, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Isabel Mauduit, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Bartholomew de Sudeley avant 1257. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1298, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Beauchamp um 1237 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Beauchamp 24 und ihre Mutter Isabel Mauduit 24. Sie hat vor 1257 Bartholomew de Sudeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1298 im Alter von 62 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beauchamp è nata il circa 1237 nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Isabel Mauduit, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Bartholomew de Sudeley prima del 1257. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1298, all'età di 62 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1237년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Mauduit(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1257년 이전 Bartholomew de Sudeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1298년 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1237, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Beauchamp, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Mauduit, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Bartholomew de Sudeley antes 1257. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1298, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Beauchamp родилась около 1237 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Mauduit, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Bartholomew de Sudeley перед 1257 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1298 г. в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1237, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Beauchamp, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Isabel Mauduit, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Bartholomew de Sudeley antes de 1257. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1298, a la edad de 62 años.
